source_metadata.title_raw: Kristen Barneich - A-19-074 - April 12, 2019 - Arroyo Grande

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Kristen Barneich 
Mayor Pro Tern 
City of Arroyo Grande 
328 Stillwell Drive 
Arroyo Grande, CA 93420 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-074 
Dear Ms. Bameich: 
April 12, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section l 090. 
Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Given that you are the uncompensated Vice President of the Board for the non-profit 5Cities 
Homeless Coalition ("5Cities"), and also the Mayor Pro Tern of Arroyo Grande, may you take part 
in an Arroyo Grande public hearing "weighing in" on a project 5Cities may implement in a 
neighboring city? 
CONCLUSION 
Because you do not have an economic interest in 5Cities as a source-of-income or as a 
business entity (given that you are a volunteer member of a non-profit), your role with 5Cities does 
not create a conflict of interest under the Act, and you may take part in a public hearing "weighing 
in" on the project proposal. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 ofthe California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
File No. A-19-074 
Page No. 2 
You are the Mayor Pro Tern of the City of Arroyo Grande. You are also the Vice President 
of the Board for the non-profit 5Cities Homeless Coalition ("5Cities"). You serve your role within 
5Cities as a private citizen, and not as a representative or appointee of the City of Arroyo Grande. 
Your 5Cities work is entirely unpaid and uncompensated. 
The 5Cities Homeless Coalition voted to apply, and has applied, for a grant from the County 
of San Louis Obispo for a project that lies in the City of Grover Beach, which is directly adjacent to 
the City of Arroyo Grande. Although the project property does not sit within Arroyo Grande's city 
limits, it is extremely close to the city limits and there has been a significant amount of written and 
public testimony that Arroyo Grande must take a position on the concept plan for the project. The 
concept plan has not been submitted in a formal application to the City of Grover Beach yet. The 
concept plan will be submitted if, and when, the County of San Luis Obispo grants the funds for the 
proposal. 
You are seeking advice on whether you may take part in a public hearing, if Arroyo Grande 
decides to hold a public hearing to "weigh in" on the concept proposal. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Pertinent to your facts, the Act's conflict of interest provisions apply 
to conflicts of interests based on the following: 
• 
An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of 
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)); or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, 
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• 
An interest in a source of income to the official, including promised income, which 
aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 
The official's interest in his or her personal finances and those of immediate family 
members. (Section 87103; Regulation 18702.5.) An official always has an interest in his or 
her personal finances. 
In this case, you stated that you do not receive income or any other compensation for your 
position on 5Cities' Board. Additionally, Section 82005 defines a "business entity" as "any 
organization or enterprise operated for a profit, including but not limited to a proprietorship, 
partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture, syndicate, corporation or association." 5Cities is a 
nonprofit organization, and does not qualify as a "business entity" under Section 87103(d). 
Therefore, you do not have a disqualifying financial interest in the City's decision on whether to 


File No. A-19-074 
Page No. 3 
"weigh in" on 5Cities' concept plan for a project in Grover Beach, and your position on 5Cities' 
Board does not create a conflict of interest under the Act.2 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
KMC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Kevin Cornwall 
Counsel, Legal Division 
2 The facts you have provided do not appear to involve a contract implicating Section 1090 and, accordingly, 
no analysis regarding Section 1090 is provided. However, we note that Section I 090 specifically includes a noninterest 
exception for a "noncompensated officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation, which, as one of its primary purposes, 
supports the functions of the body or board or to which the body or board has a legal obligation to give particular 
consideration, and provided further that this interest is noted in its official records." (Section 1091.5(a)(8).) This 
exception is more fully discussed in the Barneich Advice Letter, No. A-18-073, our response to a previous question 
regarding your participation in contracts involving 5Cities.
